channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Strip club Cinderella story 'Anora' wins best picture at 97th Academy Awards
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — "Anora,” a strip club Cinderella story without the fairy tale ending, was crowned best picture at the 97th Academy Awards on Sunday, handing Sean Baker’s gritty, Brooklyn-set screwball farce Hollywood’s top prize.
item.dc:creator: JAKE COYLE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Illinois man accused of deadly mass shooting at July 4th parade faces trial 3 years after attack
item.description: WAUKEGAN, Ill (AP) — The trial of a suburban Chicago man accused of a mass shooting at a 2022 Independence Day parade that killed seven people and wounded dozens more is set to begin Monday.
item.dc:creator: SOPHIA TAREEN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Francis off mechanical ventilation entirely, signaling he's overcome risks from respiratory crisis
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis remained in stable condition and didn’t need any mechanical ventilation Sunday, the Vatican said.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD and PAOLO SANTALUCIA, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Daryl Hannah voices support for Ukraine on Oscars stage
item.description: "Slava Ukraini!" the "Blade Runner" star said to applause as she stepped onto the Oscars stage to introduce the "Achievement in Film Editing" category.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, NewsNation

item.title: Daryl Hannah voices support for Ukraine on Oscars stage
item.description: The 64-year-old performer's brief remark came days after an explosive confrontation between President Donald Trump and Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky at the White House.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Crews battle wildfires in North and South Carolina amid dry conditions and gusty winds
item.description: Crews battled wildfires in North and South Carolina on Sunday amid dry conditions and gusty winds as residents were forced to evacuate in some areas.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Florida police search for missing 17-year-old from St. Pete
item.description: Allister Samer Nasr was wearing blue shorts, a grey sweatshirt, and riding a white beach cruiser bicycle.
item.dc:creator: Alaina Papazian, NewsNation

item.title: Rubio says Trump would be 'on his way to the Nobel Peace Prize' if he were a Democrat
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio fiercely defended President Trump’s approach to negotiating a peace deal between Ukraine and Russia on Sunday, saying the media public would be praising his efforts if he were a Democrat.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Topsy-turvy Oscar season concludes today: Here’s what to look for at the show
item.description: Sunday’s Academy Awards will bring to a close one of the most unpredictable Oscar races in recent memory.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Anti-DOGE protests at Tesla stores target Elon Musk's bottom line
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — Demonstrators gathered outside Tesla stores across the U.S. Saturday to protest the automaker's billionaire CEO, Elon Musk, and his push to slash government spending on behalf of President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: RODRIQUE NGOWI and JONATHAN J. COOPER, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Tennessee could add 'covenant marriage' with proposed bill
item.description: One Tennessee lawmaker would like to add a new kind of marriage to the books through a bill filed in the General Assembly.
item.dc:creator: Erin McCullough, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Grocery shoppers are using these strategies to combat inflation: survey
item.description: For the vast majority of Americans who don't grow what they eat, it's not feasible to avoid eye-watering prices at the grocery store. However, a recent study found that most shoppers are getting creative to save money.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, Fox 5

item.title: Firefighters working to contain wildfires in North, South Carolina
item.description: Crews have been battling wildfires in North and South Carolina this weekend, competing with dry conditions and gusty winds.
item.dc:creator: Adam Benson, KRON4

item.title: FedEx cargo plane makes emergency landing following bird strike and engine fire
item.description: A FedEx cargo plane made an emergency landing at a busy New Jersey airport on Saturday after a bird strike caused an engine fire that could be seen in the morning sky.
item.dc:creator: PATRICK WHITTLE and SARAH BRUMFIELD, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Singer Angie Stone killed in car crash after performance: Report
item.description: TMZ says per Stone's representative, Stone left a performance in Alabama early Saturday when she was killed in a car crash.
item.dc:creator: Michaela Ratliff, NewsNation

item.title: Video shows implosion of iconic Six Flags rollercoaster Kingda Ka
item.description: The king has fallen. The legendary Kingda Ka ride at Six Flags Great Adventure in New Jersey was imploded on Friday morning.
item.dc:creator: Dominique Jack, NewsNation

item.title: Tate brothers, who face human trafficking charges in Romania, are back in the US: What to know
item.description: Influencer brothers Andrew and Tristan Tate, who are charged with human trafficking in Romania, arrived Thursday in the U.S. after authorities lifted travel restrictions on the siblings, who have millions of online followers.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, Fox 5

item.title: Wyoming's only full-service clinic stops providing abortions after new regulations signed
item.description: CHEYENNE, Wyol (AP) — Wyoming's only full-service abortion clinic stopped providing abortions Friday, but it remained open after Gov. Mark Gordon approved a law requiring that such facilities be licensed as surgical centers.
item.dc:creator: MEAD GRUVER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Police kill man suspected in Platte County killings, kidnapping
item.description: The Platte County Sheriff’s Office and the Riverside Missouri Police Department are investigating a double homicide and child abduction.
item.dc:creator: Hannah King, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Man arrested for stealing $8M fishing boat with crew still on board, officials say
item.description: The suspect allegedly tried to steal a different boat earlier that morning, but its crew "ran him off the boat," police said.
item.dc:creator: John Ross Ferrara, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump's transportation department cancels new review step that could've slowed state projects
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — President Donald Trump's administration is backing off its demand for an extra layer of federal scrutiny whenever states seek even minor changes to their transportation plans, after the rescinded requirement sparked concern that some payments for roads, bridges and transit would be de
item.dc:creator: JEFF McMURRAY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: A stretch of a North Carolina highway that collapsed during Helene is about to reopen
item.description: A stretch of Interstate 40 through the western North Carolina mountains is reopening to traffic this weekend, months after Hurricane Helene's historic flooding collapsed portions of the road, partially restoring the major travel connection with eastern Tennessee.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump says he plans to add White House ballroom, pave part of the Rose Garden
item.description: President Trump in a new interview said he is planning to add a ballroom to the White House and to pave part the Rose Garden, similar to a patio at his Mar-a-Lago property in Florida.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5

item.title: US lobster catch drops as crustaceans migrate to colder Canadian waters
item.description: SCARBOROUGH, Maine (AP) — The U.S. lobster industry's catch keeps sliding as fishermen contend with the northward migration of the valuable crustaceans. The industry is based mostly in Maine, where lobsters are both a cultural signifier and the backbone of the coastal economy.
item.dc:creator: PATRICK WHITTLE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump to sign order to make English official language of US
item.description: President Trump is expected to sign an executive order to make English the official language of the United States, rescinding policies that require federal agencies to provide language assistance for non-English speakers.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, WGN 9

item.title: Pope Francis continues to improve but his prognosis remains guarded: Vatican
item.description: Pope Francis continued his slow recovery from double pneumonia on Thursday, beating back speculation of an imminent death, resignation or conclave and signalling that he was still very much in charge albeit in a weakened state.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Gene Hackman, wife Betsy Arakawa were dead for some time, found in different rooms, investigators say
item.description: A spokesperson for the state’s medical examiner declined to comment on whether any conclusions had been reached on the cause.
item.dc:creator: Susan Montoya Bryan, ABC 4

item.title: Students escape school bus fire in Ohio
item.description: An emergency broke out Thursday morning on a school bus carrying children in Cleveland Heights, Ohio. An unexpected blaze — requiring a quick evacuation.
item.dc:creator: Celeste Houmard, Fox 5

item.title: Menendez family criticizes LA district attorney for opposing brothers' new trial
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Cousins of Erik and Lyle Menendez spoke out Thursday criticizing the Los Angeles district attorney's recent decision to oppose a new trial for the brothers, who have spent nearly 30 years in prison for the 1989 killing of their parents.
item.dc:creator: JAIMIE DING, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Cardinal McElroy calls for compassion in immigration policy as he prepares to lead in Washington
item.description: Cardinal Robert McElroy, who is getting ready to take over as archbishop of Washington, D.C. in March, called for compassion and dignity for migrants Thursday.
item.dc:creator: DEEPA BHARATH, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: No new bombshells in Justice Department’s release of Jeffrey Epstein files
item.description: Attorney General Pam Bondi on Thursday released government documents related to wealthy financier Jeffrey Epstein, but the first wave of files posted on the Justice Department website have largely been circulating in the public domain for years and didn't include any new bombshells.
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER and MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: NOAA begins firing hundreds of staffers
item.description: The National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA) began the process of firing hundreds of probationary workers Thursday, the latest mass job cuts within federal agencies, three sources with knowledge of internal operations told The Hill.
item.dc:creator: Zack Budryk, WGN 9

item.title: Man on 'Most Wanted' list for over 4 decades caught in New Mexico
item.description: After more than four decades, Stephen Craig Campbell, one of the United States' most wanted men, is now behind bars.
item.dc:creator: Bela Olague, WGN 9

item.title: Betsy Arakawa, concert pianist married to actor Gene Hackman, dead at 65
item.description: Betsy Arakawa, a concert pianist and co-founder of a home furnishing business, was found dead Wednesday in her Santa Fe, New Mexico, home along with actor-husband Gene Hackman and their dog, according to authorities. She was 65.
item.dc:creator: HILLEL ITALIE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: The Tate brothers, who face human trafficking charges in Romania, arrive in Florida
item.description: After the brothers arrived in Florida, state Attorney General James Uthmeier said in a social media post that his office would conduct a “preliminary inquiry” into them.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Elon Musk urges retired air traffic controllers to come back
item.description: Earlier this month, President Trump’s administration began firing hundreds of workers at the Federal Aviation Administration (FAA), including personnel brought on for the FAA radar as well as landing and navigational aid maintenance.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, ABC 4

item.title: FDA cancels meeting to choose flu vaccine strain
item.description: The cancellation of the FDA meeting, alongside a similar postponement last week of a meeting of Centers for Disease Control and Prevention vaccine advisors, is adding to the growing concern among scientists and public health advocates that Health and Human Services Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, WGN 9
